message:
test(fcp-conformance/connector-artifacts): pin schema-namespace + arch-translation contract (br-qxepk)

connector_artifacts defines the canonical schemas for mirrored
connector manifests, binaries, and repair descriptors. The schema
namespace 'fcp.core' + canonical struct names + version 1.0.0 are
wire-format contracts that registry, store, and install layers all
key on — drift would silently alias indexing or split installs
across versions.

Adds 17 tests against the public fcp_core::connector_artifacts API:

- 4× per-schema {namespace, name, version} pinning (Manifest /
  Binary / SymbolSet / signing-view)
- all_artifact_schemas_share_namespace_and_version (cross-check)
- schemas_are_distinct_per_artifact_type (4 unique names — no
  registry-indexing aliasing)
- ConnectorTarget::from_env non-empty + arch translation
  (x86_64 -> amd64, aarch64 -> arm64; failure ensures binary
  lookup keys stay portable across host process arch reporting)
- ConnectorTarget::as_string format ("os-arch")
- ConnectorBinaryTransmissionInfo::new defaults payload_hash=None
- with_payload_hash sets the field
- serde omits payload_hash when None (forward compat with
  pre-feature readers)
- serde-deserializes legacy JSON without payload_hash as None
  (backward compat with pre-feature artifacts)
- serde includes payload_hash when Some
- ConnectorBinarySymbolSet roundtrip (10-field preservation)
- ConnectorManifestObject roundtrip
- ConnectorBinaryObject roundtrip with target + bytes

All 17 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
connector_artifacts_schema_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
